decision_log.purpose: Canonical human-readable Atlas decisions and rollbacks.

project_state.purpose: Current Atlas mode, restrictions and executable/documentary surface.

derived_projects.purpose: Derived-project continuity without mutating derived runtimes.

routing_log.purpose: Append-only routing evidence for repeated decision patterns.

decision_feedback.purpose: Optional human follow-up signal over Atlas recommendations.

session_summaries.purpose: Optional concise continuity notes across longer Atlas sessions.

local_session_summary.description: Use explicit local summaries and state files to resume Atlas work safely.
local_session_summary.fallback: Use `ATLAS_STATUS.md`, `ATLAS_NEXT_STEPS.md` and the current repo diff manually.

cross_session_factory_memory.description: Use local append-only Atlas logs to understand repeated routing and governance patterns.
cross_session_factory_memory.fallback: Rely on current validation outputs and decision log until richer history is justified.

derived_project_memory.description: Use Atlas-local derived-project metadata to preserve continuity without writing into derived repos.
derived_project_memory.fallback: Treat each derived-project audit as mostly fresh and evidence-first.

plugin_memory_watchlist.description: Any plugin, MCP or service-backed memory remains blocked until separately approved and verified.

auto_injection_watchlist.description: Automatic prompt reinjection or hidden context shaping remains blocked.
auto_injection_watchlist.fallback: Ask for explicit files, summaries or prior decisions instead.

cross_machine_sync_watchlist.description: Cross-machine memory sync remains blocked until security, provenance and failure modes are reviewed.
cross_machine_sync_watchlist.fallback: Move summaries manually if continuity is truly needed.
